segments.text: Then Job replied:
If only my anguish could be weighed and all my misery be placed on the scales!
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: It would surely outweigh the sand of the seas— no wonder my words have been impetuous.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: The arrows of the Almighty are in me, my spirit drinks in their poison; God's terrors are marshaled against me.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Does a wild donkey bray when it has grass, or an ox bellow when it has fodder?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Is tasteless food eaten without salt, or is there flavor in the sap of the mallow?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I refuse to touch it; such food makes me ill.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Oh, that I might have my request, that God would grant what I hope for,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: that God would be willing to crush me, to let loose his hand and cut off my life!
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Then I would still have this consolation— my joy in unrelenting pain— that I had not denied the words of the Holy One.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: What strength do I have, that I should still hope? What prospects, that I should be patient?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Do I have the strength of stone? Is my flesh bronze?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Do I have any power to help myself, now that success has been driven from me?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Anyone who withholds kindness from a friend forsakes the fear of the Almighty.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: But my brothers are as undependable as intermittent streams, as the streams that overflow
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: when darkened by thawing ice and swollen with melting snow,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: but that stop flowing in the dry season, and in the heat vanish from their channels.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Caravans turn aside from their routes; they go off into the wasteland and perish.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: The caravans of Tema look for water, the traveling merchants of Sheba look in hope.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: They are distressed, because they had been confident; they arrive there, only to be disappointed.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Now you too have proved to be of no help; you see something dreadful and are afraid.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Have I ever said, 'Give something on my behalf, pay a ransom for me from your wealth,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: deliver me from the hand of the enemy, rescue me from the clutches of the ruthless'?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Teach me, and I will be quiet; show me where I have been wrong.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: How painful are honest words! But what do your arguments prove?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Do you mean to correct what I say, and treat my desperate words as wind?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: You would even cast lots for the fatherless and barter away your friend.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: But now be so kind as to look at me. Would I lie to your face?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Relent, do not be unjust; reconsider, for my integrity is at stake.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Is there any wickedness on my lips? Can my mouth not discern malice?
segments.speaker: Job
